articles.title: Slate Medicines Appoints Mark W. Hahn to Board of Directors
articles.summary: By Daniella Parra Slate Medicines, a private biotech company developing new treatments for migraine and other headache disorders, has appointed Mark W. Hahn to its Board of Directors, they said. The company said Hahn brings more than 25 years of life sciences leadership experience, including serving as CFO of Verona Pharma, where he helped guide […] The post Slate Medicines Appoints Mark W. Hahn to Board of Directors appeared first on ExecEdge.

articles.title: Biogen Backs Alzheimer’s Tau Drug for Phase 3 Despite Trial Miss
articles.summary: Biogen (NASDAQ:BIIB) said data from its Phase 2 CELIA study of diranersen in early Alzheimer’s disease provide proof of concept for the tau-targeting therapy, despite the trial not meeting its primary endpoint of demonstrating a predefined dose-response pattern. articles.title: BIIB Stock On Track For Worst Day In 2 Years After RBC Questions If Alzheimer’s Drug Benefits Will Replicate In Further Study
articles.summary: Biogen’s mid-stage trial of diranersen did not meet its main goal of showing a clear relationship between higher doses and better results.

articles.title: Biogen’s Alzheimer’s Drug Leaves Questions Unanswered, and That’s a Problem
articles.summary: The presentation on Biogen’s experimental Alzheimer’s treatment at a medical meeting Tuesday was supposed to boost confidence in its drug’s prospects. Instead, Biogen stock is down 6%, to $197. Tuesday’s presentation on the drug diranersen, at London’s Alzheimer’s Association International Conference, left attendees confused about what to conclude from Biogen’s Phase 2 study known as CELIA.
